description.description: The description of the ledger account settlement.

status.description: The status of the ledger account settlement. It is set to `pending` by default. To post a ledger account settlement at creation, use `posted`.

settled_ledger_account_id.description: The id of the settled ledger account whose ledger entries are queried against, and its balance is reduced as a result.

contra_ledger_account_id.description: The id of the contra ledger account that sends to or receives funds from the settled ledger account.

allow_either_direction.description: If true, the settlement amount and settlement_entry_direction will bring the settlement ledger account's balance closer to zero, even if the balance is negative.

effective_at_upper_bound.description: The exclusive upper bound of the effective_at timestamp of the ledger entries to be included in the ledger account settlement. The default value is the created_at timestamp of the ledger account settlement.

metadata.description: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be strings.

skip_settlement_ledger_transaction.description: It is set to `false` by default. It should be set to `true` when migrating existing settlements.
